context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements the Commerce.js checkout flow: generating tokens (not using cart IDs directly), calling getLive for shipping options, capturing orders with required fields, refreshing the cart post-purchase, handling errors, and using the secret key server-side.

checklist.name: generateToken with cart type
checklist.description: Calls `commerce.checkout.generateToken(cartId, {type: 'cart'})` — uses the cart ID with the `{type: 'cart'}` parameter to generate the checkout token

checklist.name: getLive for shipping options
checklist.description: Calls `commerce.checkout.getLive(checkoutTokenId)` to retrieve live checkout data including available shipping options

checklist.name: capture uses token not cart ID
checklist.description: Passes the checkout token ID (not the cart ID) to `commerce.checkout.capture()`

checklist.name: Shipping method from getLive options
checklist.description: Uses a shipping method ID sourced from `checkoutData.shipping.available_options` when constructing the capture payload

checklist.name: Capture payload structure
checklist.description: The capture call includes all required top-level fields: `customer`, `shipping`, `fulfillment`, and `payment`

checklist.name: Stripe payment gateway
checklist.description: Specifies `gateway: 'stripe'` in the payment object and includes `stripe.payment_method_id` from the Stripe.js token

checklist.name: cart.refresh() after order
checklist.description: Calls `commerce.cart.refresh()` after a successful order capture to reset the cart to a fresh empty state

checklist.name: try/catch error handling
checklist.description: Wraps `commerce.checkout.capture()` in a try/catch block and handles errors (does not let unhandled promise rejections propagate)

checklist.name: Secret key server-side only
checklist.description: The Next.js API route (orders.ts) uses the Chec secret key (e.g. `CHEC_SECRET_KEY`) while client-side code uses the public key

checklist.name: customer.login for orders
checklist.description: The orders API route calls `commerce.customer.login(email, password)` to get a customer token before calling `commerce.orders.getAllForCustomer()`

checklist.name: customer_token passed to orders
checklist.description: Passes `{customer_token: token}` to `commerce.orders.getAllForCustomer()` (not a raw session ID or header)
